text: Meeting deadlines requires good time management and clear prioritization. Start by breaking large tasks into smaller steps and estimating how long each will take. Use a calendar or planner to allocate specific time slots for these steps. It's also important to manage the scope of your work—know when to say "no" to extra requests or renegotiate deadlines if the workload becomes unrealistic. This might mean offering an alternative timeline or suggesting a smaller deliverable. Finally, communicate early and often with stakeholders. If you see a delay coming, let them know as soon as possible, along with a plan to get back on track.
